list.name: Changes
list.comment: Finished css layout for the add transaction button

task.summary: Default task
Created a main file for the wallet tracker to run uvicorn simulations

Wrote the entire backend logic, with a fully functional authentication system using JWT tokens and bearer transport. Also created a database using sqlalchemy to store all the data with a couple basic schemas. I made an example Post class as a test but later Il turn it into being fully functional
Added a name parameter to the registration field so when a user registers they can input their name
Added a new route to verify user
Added a new class that tracks transactions by adding them to my db table with multiple parameters

Added some routes to get transactions and make some transactions along with services logic which is just functions that handle the long logic like calculating current number of shares/holdings or get all transactions, etc.
Added some routes to get transactions using filters, delete transactions with their id and return transactions with specific ids. Added helper functions in the services to have cleaner code
Removed forcing user to be verified for now since I havnt even added a verification method so for testing purposes I have set it so current user does not have to be verified
Added a way to calculate the profit for every sell so that later it can be easy to calculate total profit by querying all sells and simply adding the profit attribute that they all have

New additions to be able to calculate the total portfolio value when user opens the app and also their current profit, as in the profit from the purchase price to how much they would profit if they sold today

Changed my mind with profit instead added daily profit, weekly, monthly, yearly and all time profit rather than the previous profit idea since it was nonsensical. Changed multiple functions so they take a timestamp parameter to fetch historical prices of certain crypto and stocks rather than always getting current price to compare what the price of it was 24 hours ago to now, to calculate daily profit
removed redundant import statements for cleaner looking code
Added a new method to update specific transactions along with a bunch of documentation in the form of docstrings and comments, will add more later before moving onto the front end
Added docstrings for all the routes and comments for them
Added the basic front end template with the main directories
Wrote the basic front end Transaction page to display existing transactions with no css formating yet

Added basic way to create transactions, the plan is to create everything without any css styling and add the style at the end. Will work on filtering transactions next before moving onto creating an authentication/login page
Added a way to filter transactions by symbol, action (buy or sell) and transaction date
Formatted a bunch of files by adding proper spacing and more readable code

Added a dashboard page with stats like daily, weekly, monthly, yearly and all time profits, total value, along with a button that goes to another route that tells you what your current holdings are along with the average price you bought it at, will soon make it so next to each holding it says the profit you have on it
Each holding now says the current price of it and the profit

Added a chart system where when you press on any of your current holdings a chart pops up of the historical prices of that holding, will probably do some more code formatting and documentation in the form of docstrings and comments along with reviewing my code to make minor tweaks before moving onto the next big implementation which will be an authentication page and also a chart with the users historical profits and portfolio value
Added comments and doctests for the backend routes

Added more comment and docstrings for the backend along with a change to what api is used for fetching prices. For simplicity sake I used yahoo finance for both stocks and crypto due to its ease of use to fetch historical prices and I also tweaked the way I get price history by checking if its a crypto or stock, then making the necessary changes so the parameters are compatable with yahoo finance

Added a graph that shows users historical portfolio values over time, also fixed a bug where yahoo finance failed to fetch stock prices because when you passed in the current time it would set the start as 5 mins before, and end as 5 mins after which is in the future causing it to return none
Added documentation and simplified code a bit along with clarifying variable names, comments, and docstrings with also removing some excessive variables.

Added verification pages for signup and login, made some small tweaks with bugs that I found such as crypto pricing not fetching properly with yahoo finance returning None as a price. Will soon make it so when you signup, an email is sent to you with a link to verify just as a basic overlay before starting to work on css and styling
Implemented email system using smtp protocol to send verification link

Added a way to search for stocks, the idea is that when a user wants to make a transaction they search a symbol and out comes a bunch of stocks of that symbol with prices, used an api called finnhub. For crypto I will probably use coingecko or something like that.

Added a new route in the backend that fetches crypto information based on search, will use it later to allow the use to type in symbols for crypto or stocks and get returned a list of them that they can click, opening a form and automatically sending to the backend the symbol of the asset they pressed
Changed the api im using for stock searching to twelvedata, currently have free plan with 10 api calls a minute but will upgrade before releasing
.idea files to commit

In the frontend when you add a transaction now you can type a symbol and get returned a list of all the assets starting with those letters and display their current price and change, then you can select it and then enter the information for your transaction
Basic formating for some frontend files
User can now pick exactly which crypto they purchased and information is automatically sent rather than manually typing the information in
Wrote some basic CSS for the transactions page and also changed the filtering to be onchange instead of on a submit
Finished CSS layout for the transactions page, will work on the navigation system next
Finished css layout for the navbar and uploaded some assets to use for it, the assets are made using ai but edited myself
Finished css layout for the filtering on the transaction page
Finished css layout for the add transaction button

MESSAGE.value: Wrote the basic front end Transaction page to display existing transactions with no css formating yet

Added basic way to create transactions, the plan is to create everything without any css styling and add the style at the end. Will work on filtering transactions next before moving onto creating an authentication/login page
Added a way to filter transactions by symbol, action (buy or sell) and transaction date
Formatted a bunch of files by adding proper spacing and more readable code

Added a dashboard page with stats like daily, weekly, monthly, yearly and all time profits, total value, along with a button that goes to another route that tells you what your current holdings are along with the average price you bought it at, will soon make it so next to each holding it says the profit you have on it
Each holding now says the current price of it and the profit

Added a chart system where when you press on any of your current holdings a chart pops up of the historical prices of that holding, will probably do some more code formatting and documentation in the form of docstrings and comments along with reviewing my code to make minor tweaks before moving onto the next big implementation which will be an authentication page and also a chart with the users historical profits and portfolio value
Added comments and doctests for the backend routes

Added more comment and docstrings for the backend along with a change to what api is used for fetching prices. For simplicity sake I used yahoo finance for both stocks and crypto due to its ease of use to fetch historical prices and I also tweaked the way I get price history by checking if its a crypto or stock, then making the necessary changes so the parameters are compatable with yahoo finance

Added a graph that shows users historical portfolio values over time, also fixed a bug where yahoo finance failed to fetch stock prices because when you passed in the current time it would set the start as 5 mins before, and end as 5 mins after which is in the future causing it to return none
Added documentation and simplified code a bit along with clarifying variable names, comments, and docstrings with also removing some excessive variables.

Added verification pages for signup and login, made some small tweaks with bugs that I found such as crypto pricing not fetching properly with yahoo finance returning None as a price. Will soon make it so when you signup, an email is sent to you with a link to verify just as a basic overlay before starting to work on css and styling
Implemented email system using smtp protocol to send verification link

Added a way to search for stocks, the idea is that when a user wants to make a transaction they search a symbol and out comes a bunch of stocks of that symbol with prices, used an api called finnhub. For crypto I will probably use coingecko or something like that.

Added a new route in the backend that fetches crypto information based on search, will use it later to allow the use to type in symbols for crypto or stocks and get returned a list of them that they can click, opening a form and automatically sending to the backend the symbol of the asset they pressed
Changed the api im using for stock searching to twelvedata, currently have free plan with 10 api calls a minute but will upgrade before releasing
.idea files to commit

In the frontend when you add a transaction now you can type a symbol and get returned a list of all the assets starting with those letters and display their current price and change, then you can select it and then enter the information for your transaction
Basic formating for some frontend files
User can now pick exactly which crypto they purchased and information is automatically sent rather than manually typing the information in
Wrote some basic CSS for the transactions page and also changed the filtering to be onchange instead of on a submit
Finished CSS layout for the transactions page, will work on the navigation system next
Finished css layout for the navbar and uploaded some assets to use for it, the assets are made using ai but edited myself
Finished css layout for the filtering on the transaction page
Finished css layout for the add transaction button

option.name: LAST_COMMIT_MESSAGE
option.value: Finished css layout for the add transaction button
